mechanism_narrative: A stable or declining federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months signals accommodative monetary conditions that lower the discount rate on equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following flat/declining FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly higher than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, it signals an emerging labor-market deterioration that triggers risk-off equity positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% near cycle lows leaves limited downside before triggering
failure_condition: Following triggered UNRATE increases, 5-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) reflects broad-based consumer and residential investment demand strength that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter, with the interaction of both series required since either alone is a noisy demand signal.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 683291 and HOUST at 1446 reflect current demand levels
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS-HOUST acceleration periods fail to produce 60-day forward returns exceeding the unconditional mean by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a deceleration in INDPRO growth signals fading industrial momentum and impending margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; the same INDPRO deceleration is neutral to positive in early-cycle regimes due to base effects.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.5464 near recent highs consistent with late-cycle plateau
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration in late-cycle regime shows no significant negative relationship with 20d forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 2-year rolling window, or effect sign flips versus early-cycle regime is not observed.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in PAYEMS growth combined with a rise in UNRATE indicates a genuine labor-market inflection rather than noise in either series alone, triggering rapid equity repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 152744K and UNRATE at 3.7% describe current labor tightness
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS deceleration and UNRATE increase fails to predict negative 5-day forward returns with Rank IC >= 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or single-series PAYEMS/UNRATE signals perform equally well (interaction adds no incremental IC).

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 4%, elevated inflation triggers expectations of policy tightening that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 295.62 reflects current price level
failure_condition: During periods with CPIAUCSL YoY > 4%, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in HOUST (housing starts) over the trailing 6 months reflects durable consumer confidence and construction-sector investment that feeds into broader economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1446 thousand units indicates current housing activity level
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing HOUST trend and 60-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a stagflation-like regime where FEDFUNDS is rising while CPIAUCSL YoY inflation remains elevated above 3%, the joint tightening-and-inflation signal indicates policy is behind the curve, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as investors price in recession risk from aggressive future tightening.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising 3-month trend AND CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.33% and CPIAUCSL at 295.62 describe current monetary-inflation mix
failure_condition: Joint FEDFUNDS-rising and CPIAUCSL-elevated periods fail to produce statistically significant negative 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10) relative to baseline over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction adds no incremental predictive power versus either series alone.
